categories.title: Morning Remembrance
categories.subtitle: Begin the day with gratitude, protection, and trust in Allah.

categories.title: Evening Remembrance
categories.subtitle: Settle the heart before night with calm remembrance.

categories.title: Before Sleep
categories.subtitle: Adhkar to end the day with peace and reliance on Allah.

categories.title: Upon Waking
categories.subtitle: Words of praise and awareness when a new day begins.

categories.title: After Prayer
categories.subtitle: Simple adhkar to continue remembrance after salah.

adhkar.title: Entering the Morning
adhkar.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ وَإِلَيْكَ النُّشُورُ
adhkar.transliteration: Allahumma bika asbahna wa bika amsayna wa bika nahya wa bika namutu wa ilaykan-nushur.
adhkar.translation: O Allah, by You we enter the morning, by You we enter the evening, by You we live and by You we die, and to You is the resurrection.
adhkar.notes: A foundational morning supplication expressing dependence on Allah at the start of the day.

adhkar.title: Protection in the Morning
adhkar.arabicText: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الَّذِي لَا يَضُرُّ مَعَ اسْمِهِ شَيْءٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
adhkar.transliteration: Bismillahilladhi la yadurru ma'asmihi shay'un fil-ardi wa la fis-sama'i wa huwas-Sami'ul-'Alim.
adhkar.translation: In the name of Allah with whose name nothing on earth or in heaven can cause harm, and He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing.
adhkar.notes: Commonly recited for seeking protection morning and evening.

adhkar.title: Entering the Evening
adhkar.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا وَبِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ
adhkar.transliteration: Allahumma bika amsayna wa bika asbahna wa bika nahya wa bika namutu wa ilaykal-masir.
adhkar.translation: O Allah, by You we enter the evening, by You we enter the morning, by You we live and by You we die, and to You is the final return.
adhkar.notes: The evening form of the same remembrance used in the morning.

adhkar.title: Seeking Safety
adhkar.arabicText: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
adhkar.transliteration: A'udhu bikalimatillahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq.
adhkar.translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He created.
adhkar.notes: A concise refuge supplication often used in the evening and when stopping at a place.

adhkar.title: Sleeping on Tawhid
adhkar.translation: In Your name, O Allah, I die and I live.
adhkar.notes: A brief remembrance to conclude the day with surrender to Allah.

adhkar.title: Ayat al-Kursi Before Sleep
adhkar.arabicText: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ ...
adhkar.transliteration: Allahu la ilaha illa huwa al-Hayyul-Qayyum ...
adhkar.translation: Allah - there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of all existence ...
adhkar.notes: The full ayah should be included in the final verified release dataset.

adhkar.title: Praise Upon Waking
adhkar.arabicText: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَحْيَانَا بَعْدَ مَا أَمَاتَنَا وَإِلَيْهِ النُّشُورُ
adhkar.transliteration: Alhamdu lillahil-ladhi ahyana ba'da ma amatana wa ilayhin-nushur.
adhkar.translation: All praise is for Allah who gave us life after having caused us to die, and to Him is the resurrection.
adhkar.notes: Recited when waking, renewing gratitude for another day of life.

adhkar.title: Remembering Allah After Awakening
adhkar.arabicText: لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
adhkar.transliteration: La ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lahu, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamdu wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadir.
adhkar.translation: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, without partner. His is the dominion and His is all praise, and He is over all things competent.
adhkar.notes: Often followed by additional phrases of remembrance and supplication.

adhkar.title: Seeking Forgiveness After Prayer
adhkar.notes: A simple and beloved remembrance said after the prayer concludes.

adhkar.title: Tasbih, Tahmid, and Takbir
adhkar.arabicText: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
adhkar.translation: Glory be to Allah, all praise is for Allah, and Allah is the Greatest.
adhkar.notes: Traditionally recited in sets after the obligatory prayers.
